para.bold: Pointer to the data array stoirng lesser and retarded component.
Number of the rows in the Matrix form (currently: always size1=size2) */ int size2_; /** \brief Size of the Matrix form; size1*size2`.
Pointer to a pointer storing the lesser component.
Pointer to a pointer storing the retarded component.
Number of the colums in the Matrix form.

briefdescription.para: Writes data to an HDF5 file with a specified filename and group name.
Writes data in timeslice delti to an HDF5 file for the specified group ID in the form a timeslice.
Writes data to an HDF5 file with a specified filename and group name.
Writes data to an HDF5 file for the specified group ID.
Writes data to an HDF5 file for the specified group ID and creating a subgroup given a name.

para.bold: Class herm_matrix_moving for two-time contour objects $ C(t,t') $ on a memory-truncated window.

detaileddescription.para: This class can represent a Green's function in a time range $ t_0 \geq t_i \geq t_0 - t_c $. of tc timesteps backward from some leading timestep t0. The value t0 is not stored in the class.

The class herm_matrix_moving stores two non-redundant Keldysh components:
Retarded component $ C^\mathrm{R}(t_0-i,t_0-i-s) $ for $ i,s=0,\ldots,t_c$
Lesser component $ C^<(t0-i,t_0-i-s) $ for $ i,s=0,\ldots,t_c $.
The $ C(t,t') $ can be scalar type or matrix-valued. Only square-matrix-type contour functions are fully supported.
